accidental_death_claim_details.where_did_the_accident_occur_address_cit: I-84 Eastbound near Exit 42, West Hartford, CT 06110
accidental_death_claim_details.describe_in_detail_how_the_accident_occu:
On Friday evening, July 18, 2025, at approximately 6:15 p.m., my husband, Michael Jonathan Carter, was driving home from his shift at Riverside Manufacturing Corporation. He was traveling alone in his 2019 blue Honda Accord, heading eastbound on Interstate 84 near Exit 42 in West Hartford, Connecticut. Weather conditions were clear and dry, with good visibility and moderate traffic for that time of day.

According to the Connecticut State Police accident report and information provided to our family by the investigating officer, Michael was driving in the center lane at or slightly below the posted speed limit of 65 mph. A large commercial pickup truck towing a dual-axle utility trailer loaded with construction equipment was traveling in the left lane slightly behind him. Witnesses reported that the trailer began to fishtail unexpectedly, possibly due to an improperly secured load or a sudden maneuver by the driver of the truck.

As the trailer swerved, it crossed partially into the center lane and struck the rear driver’s side of Michael’s vehicle. The impact caused Michael’s car to spin clockwise across the right lane and into the shoulder. During the spin, the car’s passenger side collided with the metal guardrail, which buckled inward. The vehicle then rebounded off the guardrail and came to rest facing westbound in the breakdown lane. The trailer detached completely from the pickup truck and overturned, spilling equipment onto the highway, but did not strike Michael’s vehicle a second time.

Several drivers immediately pulled over and called 911. One of the witnesses, a nurse who was driving a few cars behind Michael, approached the scene and attempted to render aid while awaiting emergency responders. She reported to us later that Michael was unconscious but breathing initially and had visible head trauma and significant injuries to his chest from the side impact and airbag deployment.

Emergency medical services and state police arrived within approximately 8–10 minutes of the initial 911 calls. First responders extricated Michael from the vehicle using stabilization tools because the passenger side of the car was heavily crushed and the driver’s side door was partially jammed. During transport to Hartford Hospital, Michael went into cardiac arrest, which paramedics attributed to internal injuries and blood loss from the blunt-force trauma sustained in the collision. Despite resuscitation efforts en route and in the emergency department, he was pronounced dead shortly after arrival.

There is no evidence or indication that alcohol, drugs, intentional self-harm, or a medical event on Michael’s part contributed to the accident. The state police informed us that the primary contributing factors under investigation were the instability of the trailer load and potential speeding or unsafe lane change by the driver towing the trailer. Michael had no prior history of serious motor vehicle violations and had completed a defensive driving course through his employer approximately two years before the accident. All available information from the police, hospital staff, and witnesses supports that his death was the direct result of an unforeseen, sudden, and external accidental event (the highway motor vehicle collision) and not due to any pre-existing illness or intentional act on his part.

600 Washington Street
Hartford, CT 06106

Officer in charge of investigation: Sgt. Daniel R. Morales

Michael was transported by ambulance directly from the accident scene on I-84 to Hartford Hospital, where he was treated in the Emergency Department and Trauma Center until he was pronounced deceased.

The medical examiner informed our family that the autopsy confirmed cause of death as multiple blunt force injuries due to motor vehicle collision, with manner of death classified as Accident.
